description_long: This object is a cluster of irregularly shaped stones, stacked together to form a compact, natural-looking barrier. The stones are predominantly grayish-beige with patches of green, suggesting the presence of moss or lichen on their upper surfaces. The surfaces are faceted and angular, with visible edges and corners, giving the stones a rugged, unpolished appearance. The green coloration is more concentrated on the upper and exposed faces, while the lower portions remain mostly beige. The overall form is asymmetrical, with some stones jutting out more than others, creating a varied silhouette. The texture appears rough and weathered, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
description: A cluster of angular, rough stones with green mossy patches and a weathered, natural appearance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the cluster's elongated shape, with several stones stacked vertically and green moss visible on the upper surfaces.
description_view_1: From this angle, the grouping appears more compact, with the green coloration prominent on the topmost stones and the base stones more exposed.
description_view_2: This perspective shows the largest stones at the top, with the green moss covering a broad area and the beige stone base visible below.
description_view_3: The rear view emphasizes the bulk of the cluster, with the green moss blending into the beige stone and the rough, angular surfaces clearly visible.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with similar stones as a barrier
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with green patches of moss and natural stone veining; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough with visible moss and lichen, indicating significant weathering and exposure to moisture and outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with planted areas or green spaces, often partially surrounded by grass or low vegetation, and may contrast with paved surfaces.
